channel.title: Comments for page "Mobile Task Forces"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Mobile Task Forces"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh sorry, didnt see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the recommendation; however, that task force does not qualify as all five articles were authored by the same user. Three unique authors must write for a task force for it to be considered, as is explained in the writing guide on this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this should have MTF Tau-51, as it has been used 5 times. I can provide an icon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: Re: Mistake
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please contact a staff member from our Rewrite or MAST (Maintenance) teams to let them know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Jeager Bombers just got role in the new part of the War On All Fronts Canon, will need to be updated soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think there might be a mistake in the section for Lambda-14 (One Star Reviewers). One of the SCPs they are linked to is 4065, which is the SRA. I don't think they actually get mentioned in that article. I've re-skimmed the article two or three times now and - unless I have managed to repeatedly miss them somehow - they aren't actually in there.

(I hope this doesn't mean they get deleted, because I really like the idea of a food-based MTF. I have this mental image of a group of heavily armed soldiers in tactical gear, with face concealing helmets, sitting in some fancy Five-Star-restaurant, swirling wine glasses while talking like some snobbish, cliché restaurant reviewer.)

item.title: Mistake
item.content:encoded:
MTF lambda 12 has a mistake. It states that lambda 12 has never lost an agent, but if you read scp 3470, the log for the mtf says that l5 and l6 died-albeit indirectly
"Lambda-12 quickly exits the cave. SCP-3470 does not appear as they exit the area; however, L5 and L6 could not be found and did not respond to any radio transmissions following the egress of the rest of Lambda-12."
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-3470

item.title: Re: General use MTF Field team suggestions
item.content:encoded:
Are all the MTF mentioned here present in the written articles?
This article only write about MTFs that are present in the canon SCP universe.

The full comprehensive list of Task Forces are here
Somehow, I could not find any of these MTF even in the link above. Did you made these new MTF? Please note that a few MTFs are outdated, such as MTF Tau-7 "Jihad Gang" had been changed to Chi-13 "Choir Boys" in the SCP-1983 article.

If they are present, please click the link above to update the MTF in the complete task force list. Please note that Task Force does not have to be a MTF, it could be STF, TF, RTF. For more, refer to the link above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love the logos for each MTF

item.title: Opinion
item.content:encoded:
I feel an MTF name change is in order.

Savage Beasts should obviously be Hear No Evil instead.

Also, what about Speak No Evil? Textual infohazards?

This is not a call to action or anything, I just wanted to say it.

item.title: General use MTF Field team suggestions
item.content:encoded:
MTF ???-?? "Pro Gamers"

This Cyber division is Based solely for the purpose of Monitoring and informing the foundation about the operations of Groups of interest "Gamers Against Weed" and "Atari Arcadia" please note this MTF team was not formed until after the latter public disbandment

MTF ???-?? "Creepy Hobos" and MTF ???-?? "Hobo Train"

these MTF teams constantly work together… though the first one specializes in Urban and off the grid surveillance and the other specializes in tactical combat with covert and discreet deployment… Both of them have the most Extensive MTF field codes which are synchronized, they are most often deployed in the response to each others operations going sour.

MTF Tau-7(?) "Jihad Gang"

this Battalion sized MTF team specialties in locating and terminating, suspected instances of SCP-1983 or SCP's of that nature. they must be extremely religious bordering if not crossing into fanaticism, and be fully prepared to die to complete there mission. whenever they are not involved with this they to keep skills sharp are to aid in the minimization of the obtaining of hazardous objects by group of interest ORIA.

MTF Pi-?? "Migraines"

This specialized team of a classified composition specialties in Containing, and if necessary neutralizing mind reading SCP's

more to come if you like what you see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: added with approval from Modern Erasmus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm requesting that Mobile Task Force Eta-4 ("Begone Thoth") be added to the main MTF list. They're already on the list under "Additional MTFs", but I have created a CC logo for them (seen here).

+ Licensing information
- Licensing information

▶ Iranian Simurgh – Public Domain (Edited)
The composition is imitative of the artwork style of SunnyClockwork.

- Licensing information

Here is their updated mission & list of appearances (8 articles by 5 authors):

Task Force Mission: Mobile Task Force Eta-4 is a unit of intelligent SCP-3095-affected seabirds. Eta-4 specializes in information collection and analysis, complementary field research and support, and in extreme cases, airborne combat. The task force was ultimately charged with protecting humanity after the BE-Class "Migration" End-of-Consciousness Scenario.

Assisting In Containment of Objects:

  • EE-3570
  • SCP-4688

Action Reports:

  • Team Bird Hub
  • Join the Flock
  • SPC-507
  • The Sacred Djehuti
  • Avian Anthology I
  • Avian Anthology II

item.title: Addition Request
item.content:encoded: I've already contacted Erasmus over DM, but my SCP-4175 needs to be added to the Psi-8 page. If one of the mods could add it, I'd be thankful.

item.title: Inconsistency
item.content:encoded:
Here under the Pest Control group we have

"Task Force Mission: Specializes in tracking, containing, and exterminating anomalous vermin. Often used as a first-response team when tracking anomalous organisms. Have never lost an agent on any of their missions."

But in scp-3470 we have "Lambda-12 quickly exits the cave. SCP-3470 does not appear as they exit the area; however, L5 and L6 could not be found and did not respond to any radio transmissions following the egress of the rest of Lambda-12."

So, clearly two agents were lost there, but this claims no agents have been lost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't Theta-5 (The Bigger Boat) already qualify for an entry? It's already got 5 articles written by 5 different authors.

item.title: typo
item.content:encoded: just a random note, I noticed a mistake in the Epsilon-7 ("Forget Me Not") section

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't a "work" exactly so much as an info repository.

The analogy is an archive of the original version that I'm amenable to changing if a better one is proposed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I enjoyed this work. Not only it gathers information at one place, it also have a very good presentation for the MTFs.

But I have two problems with it as it stands. The first is irritating enough for me to refrain from upvote. The second is an instant downvote for me.

1- I really don't buy the 'elite' MTFs
– hide block

Seriously, I don't like the assumed superiority of them. The concept doesn't fit as an administrative decision the Foundation would make.

Mobile Task Forces are meant to be mobile. That is it. Some tasks need a kind of operative that needs to be moved from place to place. It makes sense because the Foundation is built around facilities, and so you need an administrative construct for those who can't be bound to a facility.

But to think that a mobile operative is an elite operative is a leap of logic. Lets get the "Mole Rats" as an example. Their primary function is to enact exploration on non-euclidean closed spaces. They go there, travel through the anomaly, gather as much info as possible, and return. Sure, this task needs a very specialized training, and this means that it isn't feasible to give everyone such training, much less when other operatives can learn other things instead. But it isn't necessarily a harder task or a harder training than what you need to keep The Flesh That Hates on containment at a daily basis.

What makes the Rats into a MTF is that you don't need to re-explore the same anomaly again and again to learn its fundamental properties. Once that is done, you can save time and effort training the local containment team to specialize in that single anomaly. And so, the Rats are free to go to other site that won't need to create a team of explorers from ground zero.

Yes, there is specialization of some sort. That also happens at facilities, probably with the same efficiency. What really differentiate a MTF is that their specialization have broad applicability with infrequent application, so the Foundation can be more efficient by keeping less people and moving them.

2- I got offended by the medical analogy
– hide block

Seriously, not totally your fault, but it really annoys me. As a health professional myself, I hate how people think of us as a hierarchy of knowledge with surgeons on top.

Nurses, practice doctors and specialists do not have the same function to begin with. Each knows a lot of things and have a lot of training that the others do not. They all got clueless if put to work outside their purview. And they all go through intense preparation, if distinct in method, to achieve mastery of their fields of practice.

item.title: Re: Additional MTF request
item.content:encoded:
Oh is it three by three? I don't believe that was stated on the page.

In that case, I'll shut my mouth for now, and come back later when it does fit the bill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
new system whereby links will always go to the intended task force rather than an arbitrary link

All previous toc links on the site have been updated to the new system.

Dear lord, big props to TSAT and the team for that!!

item.title: Re: Additional MTF request
item.content:encoded: The additionals section requires at least 3 articles by 3 collective authors. You currently have 3 articles by 2 authors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to TSAT we've built a new system whereby links will always go to the intended task force rather than an arbitrary link to a certain place in the table of contents. All previous toc links on the site have been updated to the new system.

item.title: Re: Additional MTF request
item.content:encoded: The Ambrose Fair link is dead, my dude.

item.title: Additional MTF request
item.content:encoded:
I would like to request that MTF Lambda-14 ("One Star Reviewers") is added to the Additional MTF's list, they appear on the following pages:

  • Dining Out by Proasek
  • SCP-4554 by Proasek
  • The Ambrose Fair by Proasek
  • SCP-4348 by not_a_seagull
  • SCP-4065 by Arcade0416

Mobile Task Force Lambda-14 ("One Star Reviewers") is a task force that specialises in dealing with retail-oriented locations and anomalies, be they singular restaurants or entire shopping districts displaying anomalies.

Since their initial investigation of The Ambrose Restaurant, MTF Lambda-14 has been assigned to work to combat this group.

UPDATE 06/01/2019: I'd like to reiterate this request, as it now satisfied the stated reason for not being included previously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Time to go and update TOC links, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With this hub growing ever larger, any objections to me starting to put the article lists under collapsibles?

No objections at all here. Personally, I feel like the icons could also use a resizing so they're not taking up so much collective scrolling space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hub has been updated to include the Castaways in the full list and 3 new MTFs in the additionals.

With this hub growing ever larger, any objections to me starting to put the article lists under collapsibles?

item.title: Re: amendment request
item.content:encoded: The main hub doesn't seem to mention that MTF by name though, while the prologue page does?

item.title: amendment request
item.content:encoded: Damn feds's project Heimdall is linked to project-heimdall-prologue. It would be better if it is linked to main hub of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few MTFs that are either close to or meeting the requirements:

+ show block
– hide block
  • MTF-Gamma-4 ("Blondebeard's Crew") — Two authors
    • SCP-2493
    • Secure Facility Dossier Lunar Area-32
    • SCP-3301 Testing Log
    • SCP-3609
  • MTF-Eta-4 ("Begone Thoth") — Three authors
    • Team Bird Hub
    • Join the Flock
    • SPC-507
    • SCP-3570
    • The Sacred Djehuti
    • Avian Anthology I
    • Avian Anthology II
  • MTF-Theta-5 ("The Bigger Boat") — Five authors
    • SCP-2162
    • SCP-1568
    • SCP-1373
    • SCP-1409
    • SCP-3057
  • MTF-Lambda-12 ("Pest Control") — One (prolific) author
    • SCP-2350
    • SCP-2680
    • SCP-2810
    • SCP-3470
    • SCP-3640
  • MTF-Phi-2 ("Clever Girls") — Four authors
    • SCP-3057
    • SCP-3934
    • SCP-3467
    • SCP-4041

Descriptions are on the big list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For task forces with fewer articles, these may be added to the "Additional MTFs" section on this page. Please use the discussion thread for this guide to propose new additions.

I would make a post in this thread announcing your intention to have a new MTF added, and see what the response is the related works. If your task force only has one related article and they don't really feature much in it, it probably wouldn't merit an addition to this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello. Are there any requirements to get on the Additional MTF list or do I just send a request for one to be added?

item.title: Re: Military agreement?
item.content:encoded:
I don't think so. From a practical point: given the nature of the site, any person could feature any MTF in a new article at any time, even if it contradicts, say, a different article where the MTF was wiped out / disbanded. There being no rigid canon to adhere to means that MTFs never get "officially put to rest".

If I were to think of an in-universe justification: From a bureaucratic standpoint, most MTFs are named for their function/task. So no matter how many members come and go through the ranks, it's always the same MTF designation for the same task.

item.title: Military agreement?
item.content:encoded: Are we taking into consideration the international consensus that no two groups have the same name? (E.g.: If all Zeta-9 ""Mole Rats" members died, this name would be set to rest and unused for future MTFs)

item.title: Re: The right MTF for the right job
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'll ask there as soon as i can.

item.title: Re: The right MTF for the right job
item.content:encoded: This would be something to ask in the Foundation Universe forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-88827/foundation-universe

item.title: The right MTF for the right job
item.content:encoded: Hi, can anyone tell me if there's already an MTF i could use against a CognitoHazard spread with a smell or if i should create a specialized MTF myself?

item.title: MTF Idea
item.content:encoded: I had an idea for a reconnaissance MTF to move with other MTF Units to gather intel and data from their missions. It would mostly be a data collecting, monitoring, and espionage task force with the intent of keeping tabs on Foundation personnel and GOI's. The name would be MTF Mu-16 ("Central Intelligence"). There could be some specialized agents that writers could use how they wanted.

item.title: Re: MTF Application
item.content:encoded: ….I need to go rename my MTF apparently. >_>

item.title: MTF Application
item.content:encoded:
Requesting that MTF Beta-4 ("Castaways") be added to the main list. To paraphrase the SCLoMTF, "MTF Beta-4 is a task force created with the sole purpose of assisting and monitoring GoI-466 (Wilson's Wildlife Solutions) in their interactions with local fauna-based anomalies."

They've been used in the following articles:

  • SCP-3465 by DarkStuff
  • SCP-3466 by DarkStuff
  • SCP-3467 by Weryllium
  • SCP-3577 by Blooper
  • SCP-3676 by MaliceAforethought
  • SCP-3879 by Uncle Nicolini

For a total of 6 articles by 5 authors.

item.title: Re: Updates
item.content:encoded:
The links were probably just early in the list or someone else updated the ones you saw.

No, they were links to later forces; Epsilon is pretty early in the alphabet. I checked the history and nobody else had updated any of my pages recently either.

I'm not sure there's anything I can do to fix that aside from comb the wiki and edit every toc link every time i update this page, which would be an Everest of work for a molehill of benefit.

Fixing the pages you own isn't as bad, unless you are Roget.

item.title: Re: Updates
item.content:encoded:
I am secretly the owner of Wikidot

The links were probably just early in the list or someone else updated the ones you saw. Sad to say, but lots of pages on the site that linked to a specific spot on the mtf list now go to the wrong one. Honestly, it's not the biggest deal though so its still on the same page. I'm not sure there's anything I can do to fix that aside from comb the wiki and edit every toc link every time i update this page, which would be an Everest of work for a molehill of benefit.

item.title: Re: Updates
item.content:encoded: I went to check on some toc links and found that they haven't changed. How did you do this voodoo?

item.title: Updates
item.content:encoded:
Village Idiots have been added to the main list, with great art courtesy of SunnyClockwork.

The winning art piece from the social media art contest has been added to the hub, and as there was only one other submission to the category (not including a logo already displayed here) I added the remaining piece as a banner due to its shape.

Congratulations to YugoProductions and Scorpion141!

item.title: Re: MTF Application
item.content:encoded: Yep, I'll get in touch with Sunny over making a logo for them. Once its done, It'll be added to the main list.

item.title: Re: Page Updated
item.content:encoded: … Oh crap. I hadn't even considered that. Whelp, looks like I have some wikiwork to do.

item.title: MTF Application
item.content:encoded:
Requesting that MTF Epsilon-6 ("Village Idiots") get moved up from the 'Additional MTFs' at last, with at least 5 works by at least 3 different authors:

SCP-2561
SCP-3449
SCP-3942
SCP-2815
SCP-2447
SCP-2480
SCP-3845
SCP-2477
SCP-3322

There might be more though.

item.title: Re: Page Updated
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Note that all links in articles to MTFs after Omega-Zero are now off by one (or more; apparently some of them haven't been updated after earlier additions).

item.title: Page Updated
item.content:encoded: Recent proposal updates have been incorporated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Duplicate post from ajax error.

item.title: Additions to entries
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2828 uses Eta-10, Eta-11, and Epsilon-7 and should probably be added to each of those entries.

SCP-3533 uses Epsilon-7, which incidentally brings them to five pages by five authors.

ETA: Suggestion for logo: a forget-me-not flower growing out of a pharmaceutical bottle.

ETA2: Looks like Epsilon-7 is no longer in SCP-3533

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded:
Never gave a thought to writing up a Mobile Task Task.

Now…

I'd gladly spend the rest of my time on this site conceiving, developing, and bolstering an MTF, if that is what it took to see another logo made.

Because MY GOD

Well done, SunnyClockwork.

item.title: MTF Application
item.content:encoded:
MTF Omega-0 ("Ará Orún), with five works, and one supplement by four authors.

+ show block
– hide block

[[div class="content-panel standalone series" style="background-color: #FFFFFF;"]]
[[=]]
+ MTF Omega-0 ("Ará Orún")
[[=image MTF Omega-Zero by Sunnyclockwork size="small"]]
[[/=]]

**Task Force Mission:** The "saints" of MTF Omega-0 are informational constructs with the memories of deceased Foundation personnel able to manifest through access of the Foundation's intranet terminals. Using Identity Warfare Training (IWT) they protect their living comrades against informational threats and entities. The existence of MTF ω-0 is unknown to most or all of the living members of the Foundation.

**Assisting In Containment of Objects:**
* [[[SCP-2111]]]
* [[[SCP-2759]]]

**Action Reports:**
* [[[In The Trenches With the Dead]]]
* [[[Matterminded]]]
* [[[Your Last First Day]]]
* [[[richard-larenz-personal-log|Personal Log of Richard Larenz]]]

[[div style="text-align:right"]]
,,[#toc Back to Top],,
[[/div]]
[[/div]]

Image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did not add the kinkshamers, as that actually has 5 articles by 3 authors and so qualifies for the main list.

I just noticed this apparently never happened?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting they also appear in SCP-2759 and its supplement. Currently five works by four authors, although one is a supplement.

item.title: there goes my life
item.content:encoded:
Welp, there goes my medical career. I shall be consumed by this page alone.

Thank you very much for the compilation though. Srsly.
